For most back-office and dev workflows in 2026, text-only is still enough. If youâ€TMre building something like a â€œvisual code reviewerâ€ or an â€œinvoice-to-ERPâ€ pipeline, plan on combining a vision encoder with one of these text flagships rather than expecting an all-in-one multimodal checkpoint. 9. License Reality Per Model Licensing is where many teams trip up. Hereâ€TMs the concise breakdown based on the ground-truth block. Practical guidance: - If legal wants [...] Mistral-Large-3-675B-Instruct-2512` are much more expensive per token than MoEs with similar quality. - Hardware efficiency â€“ H100/H200 vs older A100 vs consumer GPUs; better hardware can drop $/token significantly. - Utilization â€“ multi-tenant saturation vs idle GPUs; low utilization can make even â€œcheapâ€ models expensive. Qualitative ranking (cheapest â†’ most expensive per token at similar quality): - DeepSeek-V4-Flash â€“ explicitly small/fast MoE; ideal for low-cost, high-throughput workloads. - Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B â€“ 3B active MoE; very efficient for its quality, especially on Apache 2.0. - Llama-4-Scout-17B-16E-Instruct â€“ 17B active; more expensive than 3B-active MoEs but still efficient compared to huge dense models. - DeepSeek-V4-Pro / Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B / Llama-4-Maverick-17B-128E-Instruct â€“ big MoEs with higher total capacity; cost depends heavily on routing and quantization. - Mistral-Small-4-119B-2603 â€“ 119B dense; more expensive per token than similarly strong MoEs but simpler to reason about. - Mistral-Large-3-675B-Instruct-2512 â€“ 675B dense; highest cost per token but also highest single-model capacity. Comparing to APIs: - If youâ€TMre running GPUs at high utilization, a well-tuned DeepSeek-V4-Flash or Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B deployment can undercut GPTâ€‘5â€‘mini or Gemini 3.1 Flash on $/M tokens. - For â€œfrontierâ€ quality, Mistral-Large-3-675B-Instruct-2512 or Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B can be cheaper per token than GPTâ€‘5.4â€‘pro or Claude Opus 4.7 if you have steady, heavy usage; otherwise, API pay-per-use might still be cheaper. title: Gemma 4: From Raspberry Pi to Research Workstation — One Architecture, No Quality Compromise
summary: Gemma 4: From Raspberry Pi to Research Workstation — One Architecture, No Quality Compromise - DEV Community Published: 2026-05-17T02:15:34+00:00 Summary Google DeepMind's Gemma 4, a family of four open-weights multimodal models, was released in 2026 under the Apache 2.0 license.
content: Google DeepMind's Gemma 4, a family of four open-weights multimodal models, was released in 2026 under the Apache 2.0 license. The architecture includes a 2-billion-parameter model that scores 37.on AIME 2026 while fitting inside 1.5 GB of quantized memory. However, the 31B dense model scores 89.on the same benchmark. This article explains the engineering decisions that make these models possible without compromising quality. The "E" [...] E2B and E4B does not stand for "efficient" in a marketing sense, but stands for Effective. The family [...] two axes: deployment tier (edge vs. cloud) and inference philosophy (sparse MoE vs. dense). The architecture interleaves two kinds: local sliding-window attention (512 tokens for E2/E4B, 1024 for 26B/31B), which processes nearby tokens. Global full-context attention attends to the entire context window. The final layer is always global, ensuring that the [...] 's output generation has access to the [...] . This trace is stripped from the output by default — you see only the conclusion, not the working. Think of it as Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning that the model has been distilled to perform natively - you do not have to prompt it into "thinking step by step." When`enable_thinking=True`, the model allocates token budget to working through the problem before committing to an answer. When to enable it: - Mathematical proofs, competitive programming, multi-step logical deduction - Agent [...] model must plan before [...] tools - Any task where correctness matters more than latency When to disable it: - Simple retrieval, summarization, conversational interfaces - High-volume serving where every extra token costs money - Real-time applications where sub-second response is required The thinking tokens are invisible in the final output by default — the model strips them. But they directly influence the answer quality, particularly on the AIME-class [...] 31 [...] %. Agent [...] tool-use protocol [...] a prompt engineering workaround [...] because open models can't keep up." That is not a [...] about Google's generosity. It is a statement about architectural progress: Per-Layer Embeddings enabling edge deployment, MoE enabling cost-efficient cloud inference, hybrid attention enabling 256K context, and trained function-calling enabling production-grade agents - compounding technical advances that happened to land in [...] release. title: A new personal finance experience in ChatGPT
summary: A new personal finance experience in ChatGPT | OpenAI Published: 2026-05-16T11:41:15+00:00 Summary The company is releasing a preview of a new personal finance experience in ChatGPT to Pro users in the US, allowing users to securely connect their financial accounts and ask questions grounded
content: The company is releasing a preview of a new personal finance experience in ChatGPT to Pro users in the US, allowing users to securely connect their financial accounts and ask questions grounded in their financial context. This comes as part of a larger effort to improve the experience and expand the service further. The ability to connect financial accounts with their real financial context and share important context about your financial life can help identify patterns, understand tradeoffs, and plan for big decisions in a way that feels more personal and complete. The company is working with trusted ecosystem partners like Intuit to develop an internal benchmark to evaluate response quality and grade the service's performance on challenging personal finance tasks. title: OpenAI unveils GPT-4, a new foundation for ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI unveils GPT-4, a new foundation for ChatGPT – Computerworld OpenAI unveils GPT-4, a new foundation for ChatGPT news analysis Mar 14, 20235 mins GPT-4 promises to open up new use cases for OpenAI's chatbot technology, enabling visual and audio inputs. Artificial intelligence (AI) res
content: OpenAI unveils GPT-4, a new foundation for ChatGPT – Computerworld [...] OpenAI unveils GPT-4, a new foundation for ChatGPT [...] GPT-4 promises to open up new use cases for OpenAI's chatbot technology, enabling visual and audio inputs. [...] Artificial intelligence (AI) research firm OpenAI today revealed the latest version of its computer program for natural language processing that powers ChatGPT, the wildly hyped chatbot with a fast-growing user base. [...] ChatGPT creator OpenAI announced the new large language model in a blog post, saying it will have better features than its predecessor, GPT-3.5 Word of GPT-4 first leaked last week when Andreas Braun, CTO of Microsoft Germany, let slip that it would be launched this week. [...] The new GPT-4 large language model will be different from previous versions, offering what the company called a “multimodal system” that can process not just text, but images, video, or audio. [...] The other capability OpenAI appears to be touting is the ability of GPT-4 to handle inputs in several languages beyond English. [...] ” it will provide an evolved tool [...] One way GPT-4 will likely be used is with “computer vision.” For example, image-to-text capabilities can be used for visual assistance or process automation within enterprise, according to Chandrasekaran. [...] are already being [...] karan said. “And it looks like Khan Academy, for example, is launching a tutor bot based on GPT-4. In addition, we will [see a] plethora of apps being built for both English speakers and other languages. The ability to adapt to different personas could enable more differentiated and targeted applications to be built on GPT-4.” [...] OpenAI said the distinction between GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 can be “subtle.” [...] “The difference comes out when the complexity of the task reaches a sufficient threshold. GPT-4 is more reliable, creative, and able to handle much more nuanced instructions than GPT-3.5,” the company said in its blog post today. [...] “A year ago, we trained GPT-3.5 as a first ‘test run’ of the system. We found and fixed some bugs and improved our theoretical foundations. As a result, our GPT-4 training run was…unprecedentedly stable, becoming our first large model whose training performance we were able to accurately predict ahead of time,” OpenAI said.

title: OpenAI Launches $4 Billion Enterprise AI Deployment Venture, Recruits McKinsey and Capgemini as Co-Funders
summary: OpenAI Launches $4 Billion Enterprise AI Deployment Venture, Recruits McKinsey and Capgemini as Co-Funders Published: 2026-05-16T12:15:38-04:00 Author: Tom Kong Published: May 16 2026, 12:15 PM EDT Summary OpenAI has launched the OpenAI Deployment Company (DeployCo), a $4 billion subsidiary
content: OpenAI has launched the OpenAI Deployment Company (DeployCo), a $4 billion subsidiary of the company, designed to station its engineers inside large enterprises across healthcare, logistics, manufacturing, and financial services. DeployCo is majority-owned and controlled by OpenAI through super-voting shares with 19 outside partners, including TPG, Advent International, Bain Capital, and Brookfield. Goldman Sachs, SoftBank Corp., Warburg Pincus, and BBVA are also financial backers. The deal is unusual as investors are guaranteed a minimum 17.annual return over five years, with profits capped. The venture's 19 partners also sponsor more than 2,000 businesses worldwide, pre-sold into the DeployCo pipeline before a single engagement begins. The launch came five days after Canada's Office of the Privacy Commissioner ruled that OpenAI violated Canadian privacy laws when it built ChatGPT. title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemma 4, Its ‘Most Capable’ Open-Source AI Models
summary: Google DeepMind Releases Gemma 4, Its ‘Most Capable’ Open-Source AI Models – SMBtech Published: 2026-05-15T11:00:25+10:00 Summary Google DeepMind has released Gemma 4, a new family of open-source AI models, designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.
content: Google DeepMind Releases Gemma 4, Its ‘Most Capable’ Open-Source AI Models – SMBtech [...] Google DeepMind has released Gemma 4, a new family of open-source AI models, designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows. The release spans four model sizes, including a 26B Mixture of Experts (MoE) variant and a 31B Dense model, built on the same research and technology underpinning Google’s proprietary Gemini 3 models. The company claims these models outperform competitors up to 20 times their size on benchmarking. The decision to release under an Apache 2.0 licence represents a shift for Google's open model strategy, allowing developers to control data and deploy across cloud environments. The larger 26B and 31B models are aimed at researchers and developers working on personal computers and workstations, while the smaller ones are designed for mobile and IoT deployments.
